children.heading: Establishment
children.label: a Establishment
children.text: There is established in the Department of Labor an office to be known as the “Office of the Ombudsman” (in this section referred to as the “Office”).

An officer or employee of the Department of Labor designated by the Secretary for purposes of this section from among officers and employees of the Department who have experience and expertise necessary to carry out the duties of the Office specified in subsection (c).

An individual employed by the Secretary from the private sector from among individuals in the private sector who have experience and expertise necessary to carry out the duties of the Office specified in subsection (c).
To provide information on the benefits available under this part and part B and on the requirements and procedures applicable to the provision of such benefits.
To provide guidance and assistance to claimants.
To make recommendations to the Secretary regarding the location of centers (to be known as “resource centers”) for the acceptance and development of claims for benefits under this part and part B.
To carry out such other duties with respect to this part and part B as the Secretary shall specify for purposes of this section.

children.heading: Independent Office
children.label: d Independent Office
children.text: The Secretary shall take appropriate actions to ensure the independence of the Office within the Department of Labor, including independence from other officers and employees of the Department engaged in activities relating to the administration of the provisions of this part and part B.
Not later than July 30 each year, the Ombudsman shall submit to Congress a report on activities under this part and part B.

children.label: A
children.text: The number and types of complaints, grievances, and requests for assistance received by the Ombudsman under this part and part B during the preceding year.

children.label: B
children.text: An assessment of the most common difficulties encountered by claimants and potential claimants under this part and part B during the preceding year.
The first report under paragraph (1) shall be the report submitted in 2006.

children.label: A
children.text: includes a statement of whether the Secretary agrees or disagrees with the specific issues raised by the Ombudsman in the report;

children.label: B
children.text: if the Secretary agrees with the Ombudsman on those issues, describes the actions to be taken to correct those issues; and

children.label: C
children.text: if the Secretary does not agree with the Ombudsman on those issues, describes the reasons the Secretary does not agree.

children.heading: Outreach
children.label: f Outreach
children.text: The Secretary of Labor and the Secretary of Health and Human Services shall each undertake outreach to advise the public of the existence and duties of the Office.

children.heading: National Institute for Occupational Safety and Health Ombudsman
children.label: g National Institute for Occupational Safety and Health Ombudsman
children.text: In carrying out the duties of the Ombudsman under this section, the Ombudsman shall work with the individual employed by the National Institute for Occupational Safety and Health to serve as an ombudsman to individuals making claims under part B.
